seo.title: Land Rover Engine Replacement - Compare Prices & Save
seo.description: Land Rover engine replacement cost guide. Compare rebuilt, reconditioned and used Land Rover engine prices from £650 (200Tdi) to £8,500 (5.0 Supercharged V8), with 12-month warranty and supply & fit available from UK specialists.

hero.h1: Land Rover Engine Replacement - Compare Prices & Save

reviews.h2: Trusted by Land Rover Owners Across the UK

rating.summary: Rated 4.8 out of 5 by 1,081 verified customers who sourced reconditioned and used Land Rover engines through EngineMarket.
rating.basedOn: Based on real post-sale feedback from buyers matched with our network of UK engine specialists.

reviews.text: Thanks to EngineMarket, I saved over £1,200 on my Defender engine. Matched with a great local specialist within the hour.
reviews.name: Jimmy

reviews.text: Got three quotes within an hour and chose the best deal easily. The whole process was stress-free.
reviews.name: Pete

reviews.text: Wasn't sure what I needed, but they helped me identify the right engine and connected me with a trusted garage.
reviews.name: Sophie

reviews.text: Fast turnaround and excellent aftercare. The 12-month warranty gave me real peace of mind.
reviews.name: Ahmed

reviews.text: Best car-related experience I've had in years. Honest, quick, and saved me a fortune.
reviews.name: Liam

reviews.text: EngineMarket made a stressful situation so much easier. Cannot recommend them enough.
reviews.name: Tom

leaveReviewCta.text: Had a great experience? Your feedback helps other Land Rover owners find trusted engine specialists.

engineCodes.h2: Land Rover Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Land Rover engine - TDV6, Ingenium, V8, Td5 and legacy units. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click Get Quote on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Land Rover specialists.

groups.name: Modern Ingenium Diesel
groups.failureNote: AJ200D / 204DTD units are known for timing chain stretch, DPF-related oil dilution and turbocharger failure. AJ20D6 3.0 engines need uprated bearing shells and improved oil supply on early builds.

engines.compatibleModels: Discovery Sport (L550), Range Rover Evoque (L538), Discovery 5, Velar, Range Rover Sport
Discovery Sport, Range Rover Evoque (2015-2019), Jaguar XE/XF
Range Rover Evoque, Discovery Sport, Velar
Range Rover (L405), Range Rover Sport (L494), Discovery 5

groups.name: TDV6 Diesel
groups.failureNote: The 276DT 2.7 and 306DT / 306DTX 3.0 TDV6 family are infamous for crankshaft bearing failure. Strong rebuilds include uprated bearings, revised oil pump assemblies and improved EGR / turbo support parts.

engines.compatibleModels: Discovery 4 (L319), Range Rover Sport (L320, 2009-2013), Range Rover (L322)
Discovery 4, Range Rover Sport (2012-2018), Range Rover (L405)

groups.name: V8 Petrol
groups.failureNote: The 508PN 5.0 Supercharged V8 is known for timing chain tensioner wear, water pump leakage and supercharger coupler issues. Older 448PN 4.4 units suffer chain guide rail and VANOS failures.

engines.compatibleModels: Range Rover, Range Rover Sport, Discovery 4, SVAutobiography

groups.name: Defender Diesel
groups.failureNote: Defender diesels range from 200Tdi / 300Tdi to Td5 and Puma. The 200Tdi / 300Tdi tend toward head gasket and belt wear, while ZSD-424 / 244DT units are known for injector seal, EGR and VNT turbo issues.

engines.compatibleModels: Defender Station Wagon, Pickup, Cabrio, Platform / Chassis

groups.name: Additional & Specialist Engines
groups.failureNote: 224DT is more reliable than some Land Rover diesels but still sees EGR and turbo oil feed issues. Specialist petrols like M52B28 and Rover V8 derivatives bring their own cooling, VANOS and oiling concerns.

engines.compatibleModels: Defender conversions and limited UK fitment

commonProblems.h2: Common Land Rover Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Land Rover's rugged engineering is celebrated off-road, but specific engine families dominate the UK replacement market due to predictable design weaknesses. The TDV6 V6 diesel, 5.0L V8 petrol, and 2.0L/2.2L TD4 units account for the highest volume of specialist enquiries we handle. Every rebuilt Land Rover engine supplied through EngineMarket's network features upgraded internal components engineered to eliminate these original failure points, backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below is a practical breakdown of the most common failures, realistic repair costs, and when replacement genuinely offers better long-term value.

problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Range Rover Sport L320/L494, Discovery 4, Defender L663 (2009-2018, 2.7L & 3.0L TDV6/SDV6)
problems.rootCause: The original main bearings lack sufficient clearance under sustained high-load off-road driving, leading to premature wear and oil starvation. This causes crankshaft flex, spun bearings, and often catastrophic block damage without clear warning.

repairOptions.tier: Diagnostic only - oil system flush & inspection
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine oil and filter change, oil pressure sensor testing, sump removal and metallic debris inspection.
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable at the very first rattle; does not stop progression once bearing damage starts.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine strip-down, crankshaft machining, oversized or uprated bearings, and oil pump replacement.
repairOptions.longevity: Higher recurrence risk if crankshaft flex has already caused micro-fractures.

repairOptions.tier: Full replacement - rebuilt TDV6 with forged crankshaft
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit complete rebuilt engine with reinforced crankshaft, uprated oil pump, upgraded bearings, and bench testing.
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term route and the one that removes the core design weakness.

problems.recommendation: On a TDV6 Land Rover, once you hear the characteristic deep knocking from the sump, the crankshaft has already compromised. A minor flush or standard reground rarely survives more than 10k-15k miles. We strongly recommend a fully rebuilt unit with a reinforced crankshaft and uprated oil pump to prevent repeat failure and protect the vehicle's resale value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt TDV6 engine prices with reinforced crankshafts

problems.h4: Why Land Rover Petrol Engines Rattle & Stall
problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Range Rover L405, Range Rover Sport L494, Discovery (2010-2018, 5.0L V8 AJ133 & 2.0L Si4 GTDi)
problems.rootCause: Original nylon tensioner guides degrade prematurely under thermal cycling, while plastic chain guides crack under sustained oil pressure. This leads to chain slack, mis-timed camshaft rotation, and eventual valve-piston contact if the chain jumps teeth.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve cover and sump removal, tensioner and guide replacement, chain realignment, and fresh oil service.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective when handled before chain stretch exceeds tolerance.

repairOptions.tier: Full replacement - reconditioned AJ133 / Si4 engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Factory-spec rebuilt block, upgraded metal guides, reinforced tensioners, full gasket set, and bench run.
repairOptions.longevity: Best option once timing jump or valve damage has already happened.

problems.recommendation: If your 5.0L V8 or 2.0L Si4 exhibits a cold-start rattle or warning lights for camshaft correlation, replace the timing kit immediately. Catching it early keeps costs manageable, but if the chain has already jumped teeth or valves are damaged, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded metal guides is the only cost-effective path forward.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 5.0L V8 and 2.0L Si4 engines

problems.group: Injector Seal Failure & Cylinder Head Damage
problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Defender Puma, Freelander 2, Discovery 4, Range Rover Evoque (2011-2018, 2.2L TD4 Ford Duratorq)
problems.rootCause: Copper injector base washers harden and lose their seal, allowing combustion gases and oil vapour to escape into the cylinder head galleries. This carbonises the injector seats, cracks the cylinder head, and eventually leads to compression loss or piston ring damage.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - injector washer replacement & head skim
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Injector removal, copper washer replacement, cylinder head pressure test and skim, and EGR cleaning.
repairOptions.longevity: Only worthwhile before head cracking or bore damage develops.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit reconditioned engine with upgraded injector base seals, refreshed head gasket set, oil pump, and bench testing.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended once carbon ingress has damaged rings, bores, or compression.

problems.recommendation: On a 2.2L TD4, ignoring a faint exhaust leak or oil vapour around the injectors quickly turns a £800 washer job into a complete engine failure. We advise replacing the injectors with new copper seats at the first sign of weeping, or opting for a fully rebuilt unit if bore scoring or low compression has already occurred.

problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Range Rover Velar, Discovery 5, Defender L663, Range Rover Evoque (2015-present, 2.0L & 3.0L Ingenium Diesel)
problems.rootCause: The integrated EGR cooler and plastic coolant thermostat housing develop micro-fractures under heat cycling, allowing coolant to leak into the turbocharger centre section or mix with engine oil. This dilutes lubrication, causing turbo seal failure and potential hydrolock.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR cooler & turbo seal replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR replacement, thermostat housing swap, turbo rebuild or replacement, coolant flush, and oil-system clean.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only when cylinders and bearings show no internal coolant contamination.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt block, upgraded alloy thermostat housing, reinforced EGR cooling path, new turbo, and full calibration.
repairOptions.longevity: Best route once coolant has entered combustion chambers or compromised bearings.

problems.recommendation: Ingenium coolant leaks often mask themselves until white smoke appears or the engine runs rough on cold starts. If diagnostic scans show coolant loss without external leaks, assume internal contamination. A complete rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components is vastly more reliable than patching individual cooler and turbo components on a high-mileage unit.
problems.cta: Enquire about rebuilt Ingenium 2.0L and 3.0L diesel engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Land Rover
finalCta.paragraph: Land Rover engine failures rarely happen overnight - but once warning signs appear, delaying repairs almost always multiplies the final bill. EngineMarket cuts through the main dealer premium by aggregating transparent quotes from trusted UK rebuild specialists, all using upgraded components and rigorous bench testing. Whether your Defender needs a TD4 refresh or your Range Rover Sport requires a TDV6 crankshaft upgrade, our network ensures you get specialist-grade reliability without the dealership markup.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Land Rover Engine Replacement Prices Now ->
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Land Rover model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Land Rover Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Land Rover engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Land Rover Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM-spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,500 - £4,500 (depending on engine code)
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Land Rover engine prices ->

types.title: Land Rover Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points - such as reinforced timing chains, uprated bearings and revised oil pumps - for a longer service life than a standard replacement.
types.cta: Get rebuilt Land Rover engine quotes ->

types.title: Used Land Rover Engines
types.description: A tested, low-mileage take-out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available.
types.cta: Check used Land Rover engine availability ->

types.title: Land Rover Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory-spec, as-new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models like Range Rover Supercharged or SDV6.
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Land Rover engine costs ->

types.title: Land Rover Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older Defender or Discovery models where a full recon isn't economical.
types.cta: View refurbished Land Rover engine options ->

types.title: Land Rover Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Land Rover specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £500 - £1,500 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Land Rover engine supply & fit deals ->

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12-month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Land Rover model today.

engineSizes.h2: Land Rover Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Land Rover engine size explained - with the models and engine codes that used each displacement - so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: Compact three-cylinder diesel demand mainly comes from later Evoque and Discovery Sport applications. High-mileage replacements are usually driven by turbo actuator fatigue and injector seal degradation.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover 1.5L diesel engines ->

items.title: 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The core modern Land Rover diesel size, spanning TD4 and Ingenium families across Discovery Sport, Defender, Evoque and RR Sport. EGR cooler failures and high-pressure fuel pump wear are the most common replacement triggers.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover 2.0L diesel engines ->

items.title: 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: Ford Duratorq-based 2.2 diesels power Freelander 2, Evoque and Defender-era applications. Copper washer injector leaks, oil cooler gasket issues and turbo oil feed degradation create most enquiries.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover 2.2L diesel engines ->

items.title: 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: Td5-era five-cylinder diesel demand remains strong for Defender and Discovery restorations. Injector loom faults, head gasket fatigue and ageing ancillaries are the usual reasons owners move to replacement.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover 2.5L diesel engines ->

items.title: 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The original TDV6 2.7 powers Discovery 3 and early Range Rover Sport models. Oil pump pickup problems and crankshaft-related failures dominate this engine's replacement market.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover 2.7L diesel engines ->

items.title: 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: From 306DT and SDV6 through to later Ingenium six-cylinder diesels, 3.0-litre replacements cover Discovery, Defender and Range Rover applications. Timing-system wear, injector tracking and turbo actuator faults remain the main issues.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover 3.0L diesel engines ->

items.title: 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: K-Series 1.8 petrol engines mainly serve Freelander 1 demand. Head gasket failure, VVC wear and cooling-system weakness are the common reasons for replacement.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover 1.8L petrol engine prices ->

items.title: 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The standard premium petrol displacement across newer Defender, Discovery Sport, Evoque and Velar applications. Coolant crossover leaks and direct-injection carbon buildup drive most replacement demand.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover 2.0L petrol engine prices ->

items.title: 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: KV6 and related 2.5 petrol demand remains tied to older Freelander and Discovery-era applications. Timing tensioner failures and cooling issues are the usual triggers.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover 2.5L petrol engine prices ->

items.title: 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: AJ126 supercharged V6 petrol units support premium Range Rover and Sport models. Timing chain stretch and intercooler boost leaks are the most frequent replacement causes.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover 3.0L petrol engine prices ->

items.title: 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Older Rover V8 4.0 demand remains relevant for Discovery 2 and P38-era owners. Coolant crossover failures, valley gasket leaks and ignition wear tend to drive replacement.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover 4.0L petrol engine prices ->

items.title: 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: AJ133 5.0 V8 and supercharged V8 engines power flagship Range Rover, Sport and Defender V8 models. Rod bearing wear, supercharger coupler noise and oil cooler thermostat issues lead most replacement enquiries.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover 5.0L petrol engine prices ->

items.title: P400e PHEV Systems
items.description: Land Rover plug-in hybrid applications pair the Ingenium 2.0 petrol architecture with electric assistance across Range Rover, Discovery Sport and Evoque lines. Replacement requires high-voltage safety checks, battery state-of-health diagnostics and specialist recalibration.
items.cta: Enquire about Land Rover hybrid engine availability ->

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Land Rover needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement - instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Land Rover specialists.

fuelTypes.h2: Land Rover Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Land Rover engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known brand-wide issues, and UK specialist supply - from the most popular diesel workhorses to the latest electrified powertrains.

items.title: Land Rover Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel dominates UK Land Rover replacement enquiries across Defender, Discovery and Range Rover lines, delivering torque-rich off-road capability though specific families require proactive component monitoring. TDV6 2.7L/3.0L, Ingenium 2.0 diesel, Ford Duratorq 2.2 TD4 and Ingenium 3.0 diesel families account for most replacement demand.
items.descriptor: Diesel dominates UK Land Rover replacement enquiries across Defender, Discovery and Range Rover lines, delivering torque-rich off-road capability though specific families require proactive component monitoring.
families: TDV6 2.7L/3.0L - V6 powering Range Rover Sport, crankshaft bearing weakness pre-2016
Ingenium 2.0L D - modular four-cylinder replacing Ford TD4, EGR cooler vulnerability
Ford Duratorq 2.2L TD4 - workhorse in Freelander 2 and Discovery Sport, injector seal leaks
Ingenium 3.0L D - inline-six mild-hybrid diesel with improved oiling versus TDV6 architecture
foundIn: Range Rover Sport (L320 / L494, 2005-present) - TDV6, SDV6, Ingenium 3.0L D
Discovery Sport / Freelander 2 (L550, 2006-2020) - 2.2L TD4, Ingenium 2.0L D
Range Rover Evoque / Velar (2011-present) - Ingenium 2.0L / 3.0L diesel
knownFor: Exceptional low-end torque for towing and off-road terrain
TDV6 crankshaft bearing failures between 60k-100k miles on early units
DPF and EGR clogging on predominantly short-journey urban use
~75% of UK Land Rover engine replacement enquiries are diesel variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Diesel replacement engines ->

items.title: Land Rover Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Land Rover engines balance refined on-road performance with off-road readiness, though specific families require vigilant timing system and cooling maintenance. AJ133 5.0L V8, Ingenium 2.0 petrol, Ford EcoBoost 2.0 and AJ126 3.0 Supercharged V6 units form the main petrol replacement market.
items.descriptor: Petrol Land Rover engines balance refined on-road performance with off-road readiness, though specific families require vigilant timing system and cooling maintenance.
families: AJ133 5.0L V8 - supercharged performance unit, timing chain guide wear after 70k miles
Ingenium 2.0L P - turbocharged four-cylinder, coolant intrusion risk on early blocks
Ford EcoBoost 2.0L - pre-Ingenium petrol in Evoque / Freelander, carbon buildup prone
AJ126 3.0L Supercharged V6 - smooth power delivery, intercooler piping leak susceptibility
foundIn: Defender L663 (2020-present) - Ingenium 2.0L P, P300, P400 MHEV
Range Rover Sport (L494, 2013-2022) - AJ133 V8, AJ126 supercharged V6
Range Rover Evoque / Velar (2011-present) - EcoBoost 2.0L, Ingenium 2.0L P
knownFor: Smooth, responsive power delivery suited to mixed on/off-road driving
AJ133 V8 timing chain tensioner wear requiring metal guide upgrades
Early Ingenium petrol coolant leaks into cylinders via EGR cooler fractures
Higher fuel consumption than diesel but fewer DPF and AdBlue concerns
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Petrol engine prices ->

items.title: Land Rover Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: Land Rover integrated 48V mild-hybrid technology across Ingenium petrol and diesel families from 2020, improving stop-start refinement without altering the core engine architecture. Replacement work needs diagnostic reset capability and battery-health awareness alongside the combustion engine itself.
items.descriptor: Land Rover integrated 48V mild-hybrid technology across Ingenium petrol and diesel families from 2020, improving stop-start refinement without altering the core engine architecture.
families: Ingenium 2.0L P / MHEV - P300 / P400 badging, integrated belt-driven starter-generator
Ingenium 3.0L D / MHEV - D300 / D350 variants, shared block with conventional diesel
foundIn: Defender L663 (2020-present) - P300, P400 and D300 MHEV variants
Discovery 5 LCI (2021-present) - Ingenium 2.0L / 3.0L MHEV petrol and diesel
Range Rover Velar / Evoque (2021-present) - P250 / P300 MHEV petrol options
knownFor: 48V belt-driven starter-generator enables smoother stop-start and torque fill
Regenerative braking charges the auxiliary lithium-ion battery
Battery-health diagnostics are mandatory during any engine replacement procedure
importantNotes: MHEV engines share identical blocks with conventional Ingenium equivalents
Specialist diagnostic reset is required post-installation for synchronisation
Replacement stock is slightly more limited than standard non-hybrid variants
No plug-in hybrid or full electric Land Rover models dominate the replacement market yet
items.cta: Enquire about Land Rover Mild Hybrid engine availability ->

items.title: Land Rover Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: Land Rover PHEV variants pair Ingenium petrol engines with rear-axle electric motors for short-range zero-emission capability in premium Range Rover and Discovery Sport applications. Replacement stock is more limited and high-voltage safety isolation checks are essential.
items.descriptor: Land Rover PHEV variants pair Ingenium petrol engines with rear-axle electric motors for short-range zero-emission capability in premium Range Rover and Discovery Sport lines.
families: PT20P4 2.0L PHEV - P300e / P400e badging with integrated electric motor and high-voltage battery
knownFor: Electric motor delivers roughly 30-40 miles of WLTP electric range
Petrol engine engages automatically for longer journeys or higher loads
High-voltage safety isolation checks are essential during replacement work
importantNotes: The PHEV petrol block is identical to the conventional Ingenium 2.0L unit
A battery state-of-health report is recommended alongside any engine quote
Specialist supplier availability is more limited than standard combustion engines
Replacement typically involves a full powertrain unit rather than an engine-only swap
items.cta: Enquire about Land Rover Plug-in Hybrid engine availability ->

items.title: Land Rover Electric Powertrains
items.description: Land Rover's pure electric models remain limited in the UK market, but specialist motor, inverter and battery repair support is still available through verified high-voltage repair networks.
items.descriptor: Land Rover's pure electric models remain limited in the UK market, with specialist motor and battery repair support available through verified high-voltage networks.
families: EM0201 Dual Motor - Defender Electric prototype, roughly 400bhp combined output
knownFor: Dual permanent magnet synchronous motors deliver instant all-wheel-drive torque
~250-mile WLTP range targets for forthcoming production electric Land Rover models
Motor failure is rare; inverter and battery-module repair is the more common need
importantNotes: There is no combustion engine to replace - support is focused on HV battery and motor repair
Labour rates and diagnostic requirements differ significantly from combustion work
Replacement electric powertrain availability is currently limited to authorised channels
items.cta: Enquire about Land Rover Electric motor repair or replacement ->

fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your Land Rover has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your model, engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options - all backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Land Rover specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Land Rover Engine Codes - Most Replaced Engines (2008-2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Land Rover engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Land Rover specialists.

entries.title: 204DTD / AJ200D 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: The most common modern Land Rover engine code. Fitted to Discovery Sport (L550), Range Rover Evoque (L538), Discovery 5, Velar, and Range Rover Sport. Prone to timing-chain stretch, oil dilution from incomplete DPF regeneration, and turbocharger failure - often before 80,000 miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 204DTD / AJ200D Land Rover engine replacement ->

entries.title: 204DTA 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: The higher-output variant of the Ingenium 2.0 diesel, producing 180-240 HP. Used in Range Rover Evoque, Discovery Sport and Velar. Shares the chain and oil-dilution issues of 204DTD and also suffers high-pressure fuel pump failures under sustained load.

entries.title: AJ20D6 3.0L Diesel
entries.description: The 3.0-litre Ingenium six-cylinder diesel, producing 250-300 HP. Found in Range Rover (L405, 2018+), Range Rover Sport (L494, 2018+), and Discovery 5. Early examples suffered crankshaft bearing wear, though later revisions improved this.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ20D6 3.0L Land Rover diesel engines ->

entries.title: 276DT 2.7L TDV6 Diesel
entries.description: The original TDV6 used in Discovery 3 and early Range Rover Sport models. Notorious for catastrophic crankshaft failure, oil-pump housing disintegration, and snapped timing belts that often write off the whole engine.
entries.cta: Compare 276DT TDV6 Land Rover engine replacement costs ->

entries.title: 306DT 3.0L TDV6 Diesel
entries.description: The 3.0-litre TDV6 producing 211-245 HP for Discovery 4, Range Rover Sport and Range Rover applications. Shares the 2.7 crankshaft-bearing vulnerability with added EGR cooler and turbo actuator failures.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306DT 3.0L Land Rover TDV6 engines ->

entries.title: 306DTX 3.0L SDV6 Diesel
entries.description: The sequential twin-turbo SDV6 producing 250-306 HP for Discovery 4, Range Rover Sport and Range Rover. Crankshaft failure remains the critical risk, with secondary turbo actuator seizure also common.
entries.cta: Compare 306DTX SDV6 engine prices from UK specialists ->

entries.title: ZSD-424 2.4L Puma TDCi Diesel
entries.description: The Ford Duratorq-based Puma engine producing 122 HP for Defender 90/110/130 models. Well-documented for injector seal failure, EGR valve clogging, and VNT turbo mechanism sticking.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ZSD-424 Land Rover Defender Puma engine replacement ->

entries.title: 244DT 2.4L Diesel
entries.description: A badge-engineered variant of the ZSD-424 used across Defender Station Wagon, Pickup, Cabrio and Platform/Chassis models. Shares the injector-seal and VNT turbo issues of the core Puma engine.
entries.cta: Compare 244DT Land Rover Defender engine prices ->

entries.title: 508PN 5.0L Supercharged V8 Petrol
entries.description: Jaguar Land Rover's flagship AJ133 5.0-litre supercharged V8, producing 375-510 HP. The primary failure point is timing-chain tensioner wear, with supercharger coupler wear and cooling leaks also common at higher mileage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 508PN 5.0 Supercharged Land Rover V8 engines ->

entries.title: 448PN 4.4L V8 Petrol
entries.description: The naturally aspirated 4.4-litre V8 producing 299 HP for Discovery 3 and Range Rover Sport. Timing-chain guide-rail disintegration and VANOS solenoid failure are the main reasons for replacement.
entries.cta: Compare 448PN Land Rover V8 engine replacement prices ->

entries.title: 224DT 2.2L Diesel
entries.description: Used in Freelander 2 and early Range Rover Evoque models. Generally reliable compared to some Land Rover diesels, but EGR valve failure and turbo oil-feed pipe blockages appear at higher mileage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 224DT 2.2L Land Rover diesel engines ->

entries.title: DW12BTED4 2.7L Diesel
entries.description: A PSA/Ford co-developed 2.7-litre V6 diesel used in early Discovery 3 and Range Rover Sport models. EGR clogging and turbocharger oil leaks are the most common replacement triggers.
entries.cta: Compare DW12BTED4 Land Rover engine prices from UK suppliers ->

entries.title: 306PS 3.0L Supercharged V6 Petrol
entries.description: The 3.0-litre supercharged V6 petrol used in later Range Rover Sport and Range Rover models. Timing-chain tensioner wear and supercharger-related issues affect higher-mileage examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306PS Land Rover petrol engine replacement ->

engineCodeDirectory.closing: Can't find your engine code listed above? Use our full Land Rover engine code directory below - every code from the last 35 years - or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

directory.h3: Full Land Rover Engine Code Directory (All Years)
directory.label: View all Land Rover engine codes (90+ codes, 1990-2025)
directory.intro: A comprehensive alphabetical listing of every Land Rover engine code from the last 35 years. Use the search above or enter your registration for an instant match to your specific engine. All codes link to a quote request.

engineYears.h2: Land Rover Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section maps every major Land Rover engine change across the full UK model range - from the Td5 and 300Tdi workhorses of the late 1990s through BMW-sourced V8s, TDV6, the Ingenium family launch, and today's MHEV and PHEV inline-six platforms - organised so owners can identify their exact engine by vehicle year and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK Land Rover specialists.

years.preview: Td5 arrives in Discovery II and Defender, 300Tdi overlap remains
years.description: January 1998 brought the Td5 five-cylinder turbodiesel to Discovery II, with Defender adopting it later that year. Early 10P engines are remembered for oil pump drive bolt and head stud weakness, while 15P revisions resolved those issues.
years.cta: Get quotes for 1998 Land Rover Td5 replacement engines ->

years.preview: Td5 stabilises, P38 ends with BMW diesel and Rover V8 options
years.description: These years consolidated the improved 15P Td5 across Discovery II and Defender while the ageing Range Rover P38 closed out with BMW 2.5 TD six-cylinder diesel and Rover V8 petrol power.
years.cta: Compare 1999-2001 Land Rover Discovery II and Range Rover P38 replacement engine prices ->

years.preview: Range Rover L322 launches with BMW 4.4 V8 and 3.0 TD6
years.description: The third-generation Range Rover L322 debuted BMW-sourced 4.4 V8 petrol and 3.0 TD6 diesel power, while Discovery II and Defender continued with improved Td5 units and Freelander retained K-Series and BMW-sourced smaller engines.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2003 Range Rover L322, Discovery II and Defender engines ->

years.description: Discovery 3 introduced the 2.7 TDV6 Lion V6 diesel, replacing Td5 in that platform and setting the tone for a decade of TDV6/SDV6 demand. Range Rover L322 continued BMW-sourced engines and Defender kept the Td5 alive.
years.cta: Find rebuilt 2004 Land Rover Discovery 3 TDV6 replacement engines ->

years.preview: Range Rover Sport arrives, Freelander 2 debuts, 2.2 TD4 starts
years.description: Range Rover Sport L320 launched with 2.7 TDV6 and supercharged V8 options, while Freelander 2 debuted the Ford/PSA 2.2 Duratorq TD4. Late 2006 also brought the 3.6 TDV8 to Range Rover L322.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2006 Range Rover Sport, Freelander 2 and Discovery 3 engines ->

years.preview: Defender swaps Td5 for the 2.4 Puma TDCi
years.description: 2007 ended the Td5 era in Defender and introduced the Ford-sourced 2.4-litre DuraTorq TDCi Puma diesel with a six-speed gearbox. Discovery 3, Range Rover Sport and Freelander 2 engine ranges carried on unchanged.
years.cta: Find replacement 2007 Land Rover Defender 2.4 TDCi Puma engines ->

years.preview: Discovery 4, 3.0 SDV6 and 5.0 V8 transform the range
years.description: Discovery 4 introduced the stronger 3.0 TDV6 / SDV6, and Range Rover / RR Sport gained the landmark 5.0 V8 and 5.0 Supercharged V8. This period still defines a huge share of Land Rover replacement demand today.
years.cta: Compare 2008-2009 Discovery 4, Range Rover Sport and Range Rover replacement engine prices ->

years.preview: 4.4 TDV8 expands, Freelander eD4 appears, Defender readies 2.2
years.description: The 4.4 TDV8 delivered prestige-diesel power in Range Rover and Sport applications, while Freelander 2 added eD4 economy variants. The Defender also moved toward the 2.2 TDCi emissions-compliant era.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2011 Range Rover and Discovery 4 TDV8 / SDV6 engines ->

years.preview: Defender 2.2 TDCi arrives, Discovery 4 and late L322 mature
years.description: 2012 brought the 2.2 TDCi Euro 5 update to Defender while Discovery 4 and late Range Rover Sport continued their established SDV6 and V8 lineups. It is also the final mature year of the L322 Range Rover.
years.cta: Find 2012 Land Rover Defender 2.2 TDCi and Discovery 4 SDV6 replacement engines ->

years.preview: Range Rover L405 and Sport L494 launch on aluminium architecture
years.description: The L405 Range Rover and L494 Sport introduced a lighter aluminium platform with revised 3.0 SDV6, 4.4 SDV8 and 5.0 Supercharged V8 options. These years also signalled the beginning of Land Rover's modern premium engine era.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2014 Range Rover L405 and Range Rover Sport L494 engines ->

years.preview: Ingenium 2.0 diesel debuts in Discovery Sport and Evoque
years.description: Land Rover's own 2.0 Ingenium diesel replaced the old Ford-sourced family in Discovery Sport and Evoque applications. It was a major technical shift, but early 2015-2018 examples also became the highest-risk period for timing-chain-related failures.
years.cta: Compare 2015-2016 Discovery Sport and Evoque Ingenium replacement engine prices ->

years.preview: Discovery 5 and Velar launch, Ingenium six-cylinder diesel begins
years.description: 2017 introduced Discovery 5 and Range Rover Velar, alongside the first Land Rover use of the Ingenium inline-six diesel. The 3.0 six-cylinder earned stronger reliability marks than the earlier 2.0 four-cylinder diesel.
years.cta: Find rebuilt 2017 Discovery 5 and Range Rover Velar replacement engines ->

years.preview: Range Rover PHEV arrives, revised Ingenium chain spec rolls out
years.description: The Range Rover P400e launched Land Rover's first production plug-in hybrid, while revised timing chain tensioners improved later Ingenium 2.0 diesel reliability. This is also the point where PHEV demand begins to matter in the UK market.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2019 Range Rover PHEV, Discovery Sport and Velar replacement engines ->

years.preview: New Defender L663, 3.0 MHEV petrol and diesel, P300e expands
years.description: The new Defender cemented Ingenium as Land Rover's future, bringing 2.0 petrol, 3.0 MHEV diesel and 3.0 MHEV petrol power across the range. Discovery Sport and Evoque also expanded with P300e plug-in hybrid demand.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2021 New Defender, Discovery Sport and Discovery 5 replacement engines ->

years.preview: Range Rover L460, BMW 4.4 V8 return, larger-battery PHEV era
years.description: The latest Range Rover and Sport range combine mature Ingenium six-cylinder MHEV engines with the return of BMW 4.4 twin-turbo V8 power and larger-battery PHEV systems. It marks Land Rover's most complete electrified powertrain lineup to date.
years.cta: Get 2022-onwards Range Rover L460, L461 and Defender replacement engine quotes ->

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Land Rover was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Land Rover specialists.

faq.h2: Land Rover Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Land Rover owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Land Rover engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Land Rover engine replacement costs usually land between £2,800-£5,200 through specialists, or £6,000-£10,500 at main dealers, depending on engine family, damage severity and model. Reconditioned units with upgraded components offer the best long-term value, and prices should always be checked with fitment and warranty included.
keyPoints: Specialist reconditioned engines cost £2,800-£5,200 including fitment
Main dealer replacements typically exceed £6,000-£10,500 for the same work
Rebuilt units with upgraded parts prevent repeat failure patterns
All EngineMarket suppliers include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Costs vary by engine code, model and damage severity
items.cta: Get instant Land Rover engine replacement quotes for your model ->

items.question: Which Land Rover engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The 3.0L TDV6 with uprated bearings and the later Ingenium diesels rank among the most dependable options when their weak points have already been upgraded. Early timing-chain and pre-2015 TDV6 crankshaft risks are the main reasons rebuilt units with documented upgrades outperform unknown used engines.
keyPoints: Post-2015 Ingenium diesels feature improved timing chain and oiling design
TDV6 engines require uprated crankshaft bearings to prevent premature failure
Avoid early 2.2L TD4 units with original injector copper washers
Rebuilt engines with documented upgrades outperform used low-mileage examples
Regular oil changes every 10k miles extend any Land Rover engine life
items.cta: Compare reliable Land Rover engine options with upgrade histories ->

items.question: Is the TDV6 engine really that unreliable?
items.answer: Early TDV6 engines earned their reputation because crankshaft bearing failures can appear between 60,000 and 100,000 miles. A rebuilt TDV6 with forged crankshaft, uprated bearings and reinforced oil pump parts removes the original weak point and is far more dependable than an untouched used unit.
keyPoints: TDV6 crankshaft failures typically occur between 60k-100k miles
Uprated bearings and oil pumps prevent repeat bearing starvation issues
Later TDV6 blocks (post-2016) received factory bearing improvements
Rebuilt TDV6 engines cost £3,900-£5,200 from UK specialists
Ignoring early knocking noises risks catastrophic block damage
items.cta: Find rebuilt TDV6 engines with reinforced crankshafts for your Range Rover or Discovery ->
items.warning: TDV6 crankshaft failure can seize the engine without warning once bearing wear begins.

items.question: What causes Land Rover timing chain failure?
items.answer: Timing chain failure usually comes from plastic guide degradation and tensioner wear, particularly on 5.0L V8 and early Ingenium petrol engines. Cold-start rattles are the warning sign to act on quickly, because once the chain jumps teeth the engine can suffer immediate valve damage.
keyPoints: Plastic timing guides crack after 70k-120k miles on V8 and Si4 engines
Cold-start rattles indicate chain stretch requiring immediate inspection
Metal guide upgrades prevent repeat failure on rebuilt Land Rover engines
Timing chain replacement costs £1,600-£2,300 at independent specialists
Ignoring rattles risks valve-piston contact and complete engine destruction
items.cta: Compare Land Rover engines fitted with upgraded metal timing components ->
items.warning: A jumped timing chain on Land Rover petrol engines can bend valves instantly.

items.question: Should I buy a used Land Rover engine or reconditioned?
items.answer: Reconditioned Land Rover engines usually offer far better long-term value than used units because they include new bearings, seals, gaskets, known-upgrade parts and a warranty. Used engines can look cheaper upfront, but hidden wear and unknown service history often make them a false economy within months.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines include new bearings, seals, gaskets and upgraded weak parts
Used Land Rover engines have unknown service history and more risk
Specialist rebuilds cost roughly £2,800-£5,200 versus £1,500-£3,000 for used units
Bench testing confirms rebuilt engines meet compression and oil pressure targets
EngineMarket suppliers offer nationwide delivery and fitment support
items.cta: Compare used versus reconditioned Land Rover engine prices and warranties now ->

items.question: How long do Land Rover diesel engines last?
items.answer: Well-maintained Land Rover diesel engines can reach 150,000-200,000 miles, but specific families like early TDV6 and 2.2L TD4 units often fail sooner because of known design weaknesses. Regular oil changes, timely EGR cleaning and early leak repairs make a major difference, and a rebuilt engine with upgraded parts effectively resets the clock.
keyPoints: TDV6 and Ingenium diesels can exceed 150k miles with proper maintenance
Early 2.2L TD4 units often fail at 75k-110k miles from injector seal leaks
Oil changes every 10k miles help protect turbos and bearings
Rebuilt engines with upgraded parts typically exceed original factory longevity
Ignoring coolant leaks or oil vapour accelerates cylinder head and piston damage
items.cta: Find long-life rebuilt Land Rover diesel engines with upgrade histories ->

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Land Rover engine or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Replacing a Land Rover engine is usually worth it if the chassis, transmission and interior are still strong, because specialist rebuilds often cost £2,800-£5,200 versus £8,000+ for a comparable used replacement vehicle. Scrapping only becomes the smart option if several other major systems also need expensive work.
keyPoints: Specialist engine replacement costs £2,800-£5,200 including fitment and warranty
Comparable used Land Rovers often cost £8,000-£15,000 with unknown engine history
You keep your known service history and recent maintenance investment
Scrapping makes sense only if gearbox, transfer case or suspension also need major work
A tailored quote is the best way to judge the decision accurately
items.cta: Get a tailored Land Rover engine replacement quote to decide today ->

items.question: What's the difference between Land Rover Ingenium and older diesel engines?
items.answer: Ingenium diesels use modular aluminium construction, improved emissions systems and later 48V mild-hybrid support compared with older Ford-sourced TD4/TDV6 units. Early Ingenium engines had coolant and timing-system teething problems, but later revisions and quality rebuilt units with upgraded cooling parts have improved the picture significantly.
keyPoints: Ingenium engines use aluminium blocks versus cast iron on older TD4/TDV6 units
Integrated EGR coolers on early Ingenium can crack and leak coolant into cylinders
Mild-hybrid Ingenium variants add 48V systems for smoother stop-start operation
Rebuilt Ingenium engines use upgraded thermostat housings and reinforced EGR paths
Both generations still need strict 10k-mile oil changes
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Ingenium versus TDV6 Land Rover engine options and prices ->

items.question: Can Land Rover engines be remapped safely?
items.answer: Land Rover engines can be remapped safely when the work is carried out by specialists using conservative tune files that respect the original oiling, cooling and turbo limits. Aggressive remaps on TDV6 or Ingenium diesels increase the risk of over-boost, EGR failure and premature bearing wear, so warranty-safe tuning matters.
keyPoints: Conservative remaps can add 20-40bhp safely to Land Rover diesel engines
Aggressive tunes increase turbo over-boost and EGR carbon-buildup risk
TDV6 and Ingenium engines need custom maps that respect oiling and cooling limits
Remapping may void manufacturer warranty on newer Land Rover models
Specialist tuners can provide reversible maps and dyno verification
items.cta: Find Land Rover engine specialists who offer conservative, warranty-safe remapping options ->

items.question: Are Land Rover petrol engines more reliable than diesel?
items.answer: Petrol Land Rover engines avoid diesel-specific issues like DPF clogging and EGR buildup, but they bring their own risks such as timing chain guide wear and coolant intrusion on early Ingenium petrol units. In practice, reliability depends more on maintenance history and whether the known weak points have already been upgraded than on fuel type alone.
keyPoints: Petrol Land Rover engines avoid DPF, EGR and AdBlue issues common on diesels
5.0L V8 timing chain guides need metal upgrades after roughly 70k miles
Early Ingenium petrol units share coolant leak risks with their diesel counterparts
Regular oil changes and coolant flushes extend the life of any Land Rover petrol engine
Rebuilt petrol engines with upgraded timing parts offer stronger long-term reliability
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Land Rover petrol engine prices with upgraded timing components ->

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover engine replacement cost UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: Land Rover engine replacement usually costs £2,800-£5,200 from specialists, or £6,000-£10,500 at main dealers, depending on engine family and model. Reconditioned units with upgraded components usually offer the best long-term value. Prices vary by engine code, fitment and damage severity. Get instant Land Rover engine replacement quotes for your model.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The later Ingenium diesels and properly uprated TDV6 rebuilds rank among the strongest choices. Reliability depends less on the badge and more on whether the known weak points - timing chains, bearings, injectors or cooling parts - have already been upgraded. Compare reliable Land Rover engine options with documented upgrade histories.

mainEntity.name: Is the TDV6 engine really that unreliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Early TDV6 engines earned their reputation because crankshaft bearing failures can appear between 60,000 and 100,000 miles. The underlying design becomes far more dependable when rebuilt with forged crankshaft options, uprated bearings and revised oil pump parts. Find rebuilt TDV6 engines with reinforced crankshafts for your Range Rover or Discovery.

mainEntity.name: What causes Land Rover timing chain failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain failures usually stem from guide wear and weakened tensioners, especially on 5.0 V8 and early Ingenium petrol engines. Cold-start rattles are the warning sign to act on quickly, because a jumped chain can bend valves and destroy the engine. Compare Land Rover engines fitted with upgraded metal timing components.

mainEntity.name: Should I buy a used Land Rover engine or reconditioned?

acceptedAnswer.text: Reconditioned Land Rover engines usually offer better long-term value than used units with unknown history. A proper rebuild includes new bearings, seals, gaskets and upgraded weak components, plus a warranty and bench testing. Compare used versus reconditioned Land Rover engine prices and warranties now.

mainEntity.name: How long do Land Rover diesel engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: Well-maintained Land Rover diesel engines can reach 150,000-200,000 miles, but known weak families such as early TDV6 and some 2.2 TD4 units often fail earlier if warning signs are ignored. Regular oil changes and prompt attention to leaks make a major difference. Find long-life rebuilt Land Rover diesel engines with upgrade histories.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Land Rover engine or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Engine replacement is usually worth it if the chassis, transmission and interior are still strong. A specialist rebuild often costs far less than replacing the whole vehicle with another used Land Rover carrying an unknown engine history. Get a tailored Land Rover engine replacement quote to decide today.

mainEntity.name: What's the difference between Land Rover Ingenium and older diesel engines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Ingenium diesels bring newer emissions hardware, modular aluminium construction and mild-hybrid compatibility, while older TD4 / TDV6 units rely on earlier Ford-derived architecture. Early Ingenium engines also introduced fresh cooling and EGR weaknesses that good rebuilds now address. Compare rebuilt Ingenium versus TDV6 Land Rover engine options and prices.

mainEntity.name: Can Land Rover engines be remapped safely?

acceptedAnswer.text: Land Rover engines can be remapped safely when the tune is conservative and the specialist understands the engine's oiling, cooling and turbo limits. Aggressive remaps on TDV6 or Ingenium diesels can accelerate wear on already-sensitive components. Find Land Rover engine specialists who offer conservative, warranty-safe remapping options.

mainEntity.name: Are Land Rover petrol engines more reliable than diesel?

acceptedAnswer.text: Petrol engines avoid DPF and AdBlue issues, but they bring their own timing chain and cooling-system risks, especially on the 5.0 V8 and early Ingenium petrol family. Reliability depends more on maintenance history and upgraded weak points than on fuel type alone. Compare rebuilt Land Rover petrol engine prices with upgraded timing components.
